title: Apply Place Value Scaling in Decimal Products
description: After multiplying whole-number equivalents, adjust the product by multiplying by powers of 0.1 to account for the decimal places in the original factors.
condition: Multiplying decimals by first converting them to whole numbers (e.g., 0.28 = 28 × 0.01 or 2.1 = 21 × 0.1) and having computed the whole-number product.
response: Multiply the whole-number product by the appropriate power of 0.1 (e.g., 0.01 or 0.1 × 0.1) to obtain the correct decimal product.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires understanding of powers of ten and place value structure.

title: Compare Decimals Using Place Value
description: Compare two decimals by examining digits from left to right (ones, tenths, hundredths, thousandths) and determine which is greater based on the first place where they differ.
condition: Given two decimals to the thousandths place.
response: Determine which decimal is greater or smaller by comparing corresponding place value digits from left to right.
label: Principle / Model
example: Compare $0.439$ and $0.441$. Ones: 0 = 0. Tenths: 4 = 4. Hundredths: 3 < 4, so $0.439 < 0.441$.
notes: Supports ordering multiple decimals by repeated pairwise comparison.

title: Convert Decimal to Fraction with Denominator 1000
description: Express a decimal to the thousandths place as a fraction with denominator 1000 by using its place value.
condition: Given a decimal to the thousandths place.
response: Write the decimal as an equivalent fraction over 1000.
label: Principle / Model

title: Divide by One-Tenth
description: Recognize that dividing by 0.1 is equivalent to multiplying by 10.
condition: Given a division expression where the divisor is 0.1.
response: Multiply the dividend by 10 to find the quotient.
label: Principle / Model

title: Divide Decimal by Whole Number
description: Compute the quotient when dividing a decimal by a whole number using place value reasoning.
condition: Given a division expression with a decimal dividend and a whole number divisor.
response: Divide using place value reasoning or standard algorithm to find the decimal quotient.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Value of a Digit in a Decimal
description: Determine the value represented by a specific digit based on its place in a decimal number.
condition: Given a decimal number and a specified digit within it.
response: State or compute the value of the digit using its place value.
label: Principle / Model

title: Interpret Decimal as Fractional Place Value
description: Recognize and express a terminating decimal as a fraction with denominator 10 or 100 based on place value.
condition: Given a terminating decimal with one or two decimal places (e.g., 0.3, 0.28, 2.1, 7.3).
response: Express the decimal as tenths or hundredths (e.g., 0.3 = 3/10, 0.28 = 28/100, 2.1 = 21/10).
label: Principle / Model
example: $0.3 = \frac{3}{10}$ and $0.28 = \frac{28}{100}$ because the digits represent tenths and hundredths.

title: Interpret Decimal as Hundredths
description: Interpret a decimal to the hundredths place as a count of hundredths.
condition: A decimal with two decimal places (e.g., 0.72) is given.
response: Express the decimal as an equivalent number of hundredths (e.g., 72 hundredths).
label: Principle / Model

title: Interpret Decimal on 10×10 Grid Model
description: Determine the decimal represented by a shaded 10×10 grid by counting tenths, hundredths, and thousandths.
condition: Given a base-10 grid model with shaded parts representing tenths, hundredths, or thousandths.
response: Count shaded units and express the total as a decimal.
label: Production / Skill
example: If 6 tenths, 3 hundredths, and 1 thousandth are shaded, the decimal is $0.631$.
notes: Interpreting visual area models is often intuitive and procedural.

title: Interpret Division as Number of Groups
description: Interpret a division expression as finding how many groups of the divisor fit into the dividend.
condition: A division expression of the form a ÷ b is given.
response: Interpret the quotient as the number of groups of size b that fit into a.
label: Principle / Model
example: Interpret $1 \div 0.05$ as: How many groups of $0.05$ are in $1$?
notes: Supports diagram reasoning with unit squares.

title: Interpret Thousandths in Word Form
description: Translate a decimal written in thousandths word form into standard decimal notation.
condition: Given a number stated as "___ and ___ thousandths."
response: Write the decimal with three digits to the right of the decimal point representing thousandths.
label: Principle / Model

title: Multiply Decimals Using Place Value
description: Multiply decimal numbers by multiplying as whole numbers and placing the decimal based on total decimal places or by interpreting as fractions.
condition: Given a multiplication expression involving decimals.
response: Multiply the factors and place the decimal correctly based on place value reasoning.
label: Principle / Model
example: $1.6 \times 0.7 = 1.12$ because $16 \times 7 = 112$ and there are two decimal places total.

title: Read Decimal in Word Form
description: Express a decimal in words by naming the whole number part and the fractional part using place value language (tenths, hundredths, thousandths).
condition: Given a decimal to the thousandths place.
response: State the decimal in words using correct place value terms.
label: Rule / Plan
example: $0.631$ is read as "six hundred thirty-one thousandths."
notes: Conventional language pattern; must match place value naming.

title: Recognize Common Decimal Unit Fractions
description: Recognize standard decimal equivalents of unit fractions such as tenths and hundredths.
condition: A decimal such as 0.1 or 0.01 is encountered.
response: State its value as a fraction or place value unit (e.g., 0.1 is one tenth; 0.01 is one hundredth).
label: Concept

title: Recognize Equivalent Division Expressions
description: Identify when two division expressions are equivalent because the dividend and divisor are scaled by the same factor.
condition: Two division expressions where both dividend and divisor may differ by powers of 10.
response: Determine whether both numbers were multiplied (or divided) by the same factor to assess equivalence.
label: Concept
example: $8.4 \div 0.01 = 840 \div 1$ because both numbers are multiplied by $100$.
notes: Response is a determination of equivalence (yes/no).

title: Regroup Across Decimal Places
description: When subtracting decimals, regroup 1 whole as 10 tenths or 1 tenth as 10 hundredths when needed.
condition: In decimal subtraction, a digit in a place value is smaller than the digit being subtracted from it.
response: Regroup from the next greater place value and adjust the digits before subtracting.
label: Principle / Model
example: In $38.12 - 27.30$, regroup 1 tenth as 10 hundredths so $12 - 0$ and $1$ tenth becomes $0$ tenths after regrouping.

title: Represent Decimal Multiplication as Fraction Product
description: Rewrite a decimal multiplication expression as a product of fractions or whole numbers times powers of 0.1.
condition: Given a multiplication expression involving decimals (e.g., 0.3 × 0.5 or 2.1 × 7.3).
response: Rewrite each decimal as a fraction or whole number times a power of 0.1 and express the product accordingly.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Supports conceptual understanding of why decimal products have more decimal places.

title: Round Decimal Using Next Digit Rule
description: Round a decimal to a specified place by examining the digit to the right; if it is 5 or more, increase the rounding place by 1, otherwise keep it the same.
condition: Given a decimal and a specified rounding place (e.g., nearest tenth or hundredth).
response: Check the digit to the right of the rounding place and adjust the rounding digit accordingly, truncating remaining digits.
label: Principle / Model
example: Round $13.728$ to the nearest tenth. The hundredths digit is 2 (<5), so the result is $13.7$.
notes: Applies to rounding to tenths or hundredths.

title: Scale Dividend and Divisor by Same Factor
description: Multiply both dividend and divisor by the same power of 10 to create an equivalent division expression with a whole-number divisor.
condition: A division expression with a decimal divisor (e.g., 84 ÷ 0.1 or 1 ÷ 0.05).
response: Multiply both numbers by the same power of 10 to make the divisor a whole number, then divide.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Students must scale both numbers by the same factor.

title: Subtract Decimals by Aligning Place Values
description: Subtract two decimal numbers by aligning digits by place value and regrouping as needed.
condition: Given a subtraction expression with two decimal numbers.
response: Align decimal points, subtract digits by place value from right to left, regroup as needed, and write the difference with the decimal aligned.
label: Principle / Model

title: Use Equivalent Decimals with Trailing Zeros
description: Recognize that adding a trailing zero to a decimal does not change its value and can be used to align place values.
condition: A decimal has fewer digits to the right of the decimal point than another decimal in an addition or subtraction problem.
response: Rewrite the decimal with one or more trailing zeros to match place values.
label: Principle / Model
example: $27.3 = 27.30$ so that $38.12 - 27.30$ can be computed by place value.

title: Write Decimal in Expanded Form
description: Express a decimal as a sum of products of each digit and its place value.
condition: Given a decimal number.
response: Write it as a sum of each digit multiplied by its place value (ones, tenths, hundredths, thousandths, etc.).
label: Principle / Model
